title: Aggregation does not guard against NaN goal values
evidence: query_engine.py:154 goals_for += gf where gf comes from pd.to_numeric(..., errors='coerce') (data_loader.py:69) — a real row with a missing/non-numeric score yields NaN, so goals_for/goals_against become NaN and the NaN>NaN/NaN==NaN comparisons (query_engine.py:157-162) silently count the row as a loss. Fixture tests use clean integer goals so this is never exercised.
suggestion: Drop or fillna rows with NaN home_goal/away_goal before tallying wins/draws/losses and goals.

title: Code quality below clean (code_quality=0.667)
evidence: scores.json code_quality=0.6666 (ruff-based scorer; .ruff_cache present in run_dir). Specific warnings not re-run per skill guidance.
suggestion: Run ruff and clear the remaining warnings.

title: get_standings recomputes get_team_stats per team (O(teams × matches))
evidence: query_engine.py:215-220 loops every team and calls get_team_stats, each of which re-scans all matches via iterrows() (query_engine.py:144). For a full Brasileirao season this is ~20× re-scan of the match frame.
suggestion: Compute standings in a single vectorized/grouped pass over the season's matches.

title: Competition filter expects internal keys, not human-readable names
evidence: find_matches/get_team_stats compare competition == 'brasileirao'|'cup'|'libertadores'|'historical'|'extended' (query_engine.py:51, query_engine.py:128); the spec's natural-language examples use 'Copa do Brasil', 'Libertadores'. No mapping from display names is provided.
suggestion: Accept/normalize human-readable competition names or document the accepted keys in the tool descriptions.

title: Aggregation uses iterrows() instead of vectorized pandas
evidence: query_engine.py:86, query_engine.py:144 iterate row-by-row; against the largest CSVs (BR-Football 10,296 rows, fifa 18,207 players) this is slower than vectorized ops, though within the spec's <2s/<5s targets for filtered queries.
suggestion: Vectorize the win/loss/goal tallies with boolean masks and sums.
